primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility for hedge accounting of foreign currency risk on an intragroup dividend receivable in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Defines the exception allowing the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements and sets the qualifying criteria for hedge accounting.
Confirms the exception for intragroup monetary items and the requirement that only external‑party items can be hedged, with this specific carve‑out.
Addresses the treatment of exchange differences on intragroup monetary items that are not eliminated on consolidation, supporting the hedge‑eligibility exception.

supporting_authority.reason: Describes the types of hedges (fair value and cash flow) and the accounting treatment, which is needed once eligibility is established.
Provides the detailed accounting for fair value hedges and the recognition of gains/losses on the hedging instrument and the hedged item.

Offers guidance on hedge accounting for net investment in foreign operations, illustrating the application of hedge accounting for foreign‑currency risk, though it does not directly address intragroup monetary items.

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosures about interests in other entities are unrelated to hedge‑accounting eligibility.
Related‑party disclosure requirements do not affect hedge‑accounting treatment.
Hedge‑accounting disclosures are outside the scope of eligibility determination.
Presentation of OCI does not bear on the question of hedge eligibility.
Hyperinflation accounting is not relevant to intragroup foreign‑currency hedge eligibility.
Dividend distribution guidance does not address hedge accounting.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: Hedge accounting permissible for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under the explicit exception in IAS 39/IFRS 9 and IAS 21, the foreign‑currency risk of an intragroup monetary item (such as a dividend receivable) may be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements, provided the hedge meets the standard qualifying criteria.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for intragroup items in consolidated statements under the general rule
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The general rule restricts hedge accounting to items involving external parties; intragroup items are eliminated on consolidation and do not qualify for hedge accounting unless the specific exception for foreign‑currency risk of intragroup monetary items applies.
